gemini_interpreted_response:

The raw content is a long string of text, which appears to be the full HTML content of the page, including navigation, headers, footers, and the main body of the documentation.

The length of the retrieved content is 15,221 characters.

Nothing appeared to be missing or truncated. The browse tool successfully fetched the entire page content.

The structure of the content is that of a typical web documentation page. It includes:
*   A header with the title "URL context | Gemini API | Google AI for Developers", navigation links (e.g., "Skip to main content", "Get API key", "Cookbook", "Community"), and language/theme selectors.
*   A sidebar navigation menu with links to various sections of the Gemini API documentation (e.g., "Get started", "Models", "Core capabilities", "Tools and agents", "Guides").
*   The main content area, which is specifically about the "URL context" tool. This section details "How it works", "Combining with other tools", "Grounding with search", "Understanding the response", "Safety checks", "Token count", "Supported models", "Best Practices", "Limitations", and "Supported and unsupported content types".
*   Code examples in Python, Javascript, and REST demonstrate how to use the URL context tool.
*   A footer with links to "Release notes", "Deprecations", "Terms Privacy", and "Manage cookies".
*   The page also mentions that it uses cookies and has a "Last updated 2026-03-03 UTC" timestamp.
